India's Bandhan Bank to sell bad loans, write-offs via bid process
data.description:
(Reuters) -India's Bandhan Bank said on Thursday it will sell off its non-performing assets and written-off portfolios through a bidding process with asset reconstruction companies.

As of end-September, the lender's NPA portfolio had an outstanding principal worth 32.12 billion rupees ($359.4 million) and a written-off loan portfolio worth 37.19 billion rupees.

($1 = 89.3700 Indian rupees)

(Reporting by Hritam Mukherjee in Bengaluru)

Canada 3Q Current Account Deficit Narrows to C$9.72 Billion as Exports Pick Up
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart


OTTAWA--Canada's run of current-account deficits continued in the third quarter, though the shortfall narrowed sharply thanks to a recovery in exports following the slump seen in the wake of U.S. tariffs.

The country's current account, the broadest indicator of Canada's trading and investing relationship with other nations, logged a deficit of 13.64 billion Canadian dollars, the equivalent of about $9.72 billion, in the three months through September, Statistics Canada said Thursday.

Economists were expected the current-account gap to narrow to about C$15.34 billion, according to economists at TD Securities.

The country has recorded deficits in each of the last 13 quarters, though a sharp pull back in exports saw the shortfall balloon to a record C$25.09 billion deficit in the second quarter of 2025, revised from an earlier estimate of C$21.16 billion.

Canada's trade in goods saw a deficit of C$11.1 billion for the recent quarter, narrowed from a record C$18.44 billion deficit in the prior period, the data agency said. The longrunning surplus the country carries with the U.S. widened to roughly C$20.7 billion from C$10.9 billion the quarter before, even as the goods-trade deficit with all other countries widened to about C$31.8 billion from C$29.4 billion.

Trade between Canada and its closest neighbor and biggest market has squeezed this year following the Trump administration's introduced a series of import tariffs.

The Bank of Canada has forecast the economy will remain weak in the second half of 2025, with household and government spending helping counter the decline in exports and business investment. Data in recent months has shown that even as sectors targeted by U.S. tariffs, including automobiles, steel, aluminum and lumber, have been severely hit, the drop in demand in the U.S. for Canadian goods and services has also weighed on the rest of the economy.

Canadian exports of goods rose 1.8% for the quarter to almost C$186 billion following the 12.6% drop in shipment in the second quarter, with increased exports of crude oil and consumer goods partially offset by a decline in shipments of gold.

Imports of goods for the quarter declined 2.0% to C$197.1 billion, a second consecutive quarterly decline thanks to large drop in shipments of industrial machinery after a one-time high-value import recorded in the second quarter wasn't repeated.

Canadian exports of services increased 1.8% to C$59.77 billion, while services imports dipped 0.3% to C$59.23 billion.

Also in the third quarter, the country's investment-income surplus widened by C$2.85 billion to C$7.02 billion.

Foreign direct investment in Canada totaled roughly C$18.2 billion, taking the year-to-date value of investment to C$72.4 billion. Canadian direct investment abroad hit about C$25.1 billion the quarter, for a total of C$60.7 billion since the start of the year.


Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-27-25 0957ET

Italy's flagship MPS-Mediobanca bank takeover hit by judicial probe
data.description:
MILAN (Reuters) -The CEO of Italian bank Monte dei Paschi di Siena (MPS) and its top two shareholders are under investigation in Milan in relation to the Tuscan bank's takeover of Mediobanca , two judicial sources told Reuters on Thursday.

Milan prosecutors are probing allegations of market rigging and obstruction of regulators in relation to the acquisition, the source said, confirming an earlier report in Italian newspaper Corriere della Sera.

Shares in MPS fell by as much as more 6% on the news.

MPS, which was bailed out by the state in 2017, in September secured control of merchant bank Mediobanca with a 16-billion-euro ($19 billion) stock-and-cash bid in a takeover championed by the Italian government.

According to prosecutors, Italy's market watchdog Consob, the European Central Bank and insurance supervisor Ivass were kept in the dark about concerted action involving the bank and its leading investors over the Mediobanca bid, the sources said.

The ECB declined to comment.

MPS has been led since early 2022 by CEO Luigi Lovaglio, 70, a former veteran UniCredit executive.

Its biggest shareholders are Francesco Gaetano Caltagirone, a construction magnate, and Delfin, the holding company of Italy's Del Vecchio family, which is led by EssilorLuxottica CEO Francesco Milleri.

SUSPECTS UNDER INVESTIGATION SINCE JUNE

Lovaglio, Caltagirone and Milleri have all been under investigation since June, the sources said, but their names have only now emerged after the prosecutors began searches in connection with their investigation.

A representative for Milleri declined to comment. MPS had no immediate comment while it was not immediately possible to reach a representative for Caltagirone.

The offices of MPS have been searched this week, said the sources, who asked not to be named.

The Milan prosecutors in charge of the case are Roberto Pellicano, Luca Gaglio e Giovanni Polizzi.

MPS announced its bid for Mediobanca in January after the Treasury completed its re-privatisation of the bailed-out bank, cutting its initial 68% stake to a near 12% holding which has now more than halved with the Mediobanca deal.

Caltagirone and Delfin were also the top investors in Mediobanca and played a key role in supporting the bid.

($1 = 0.8631 euros)

(Reporting by Alvise Armellini, Gianluca Semeraro and Angelo Amante, editing by Alvise Armellini, Valentina Za and Gavin Jones)

By Valentina Za and Alvise Armellini

Caltagirone, Milleri, and Lovaglio Under Investigation in MPS-Mediobanca Takeover Probe
data.description:
(Alliance News) - On Thursday, Corriere della Sera reported that entrepreneur Francesco Gaetano Caltagirone, Luxottica and Luxembourg-based holding Delfin chairman Francesco Milleri, and Monte dei Paschi di Siena CEO Luigi Lovaglio are under investigation by the Milan Prosecutor's Office on suspicion of market manipulation and obstruction of regulatory authorities. According to investigators, they allegedly orchestrated the EUR13.5 billion public exchange offer (OPS), which secured a 62% participation rate on the stock exchange.

Through this operation, between January and October of this year, MPS – in which the state was the main shareholder – gained control of Mediobanca, the leading shareholder in Generali with a 13.2% stake. Generali already includes significant holdings attributable to both Caltagirone and Delfin.

The newspaper further specifies that the Caltagirone group and Delfin, as legal entities, are also under investigation by the economic crimes unit led by Deputy Prosecutor Roberto Pellicano. This is pursuant to Law 231/2001, which governs corporate administrative liability for crimes committed by top management in the interest of the company.

The agreement – which was not disclosed to the market as required by regulations – concerning the takeover of Mediobanca was allegedly concealed not only from the public but also from supervisory authorities: Consob for the financial markets, the European Central Bank (ECB) for the banking system, and Ivass for the insurance sector.

According to investigators, as reported by the newspaper, this agreement entailed coordinated purchases of MPS shares – including those sold by the Treasury at the end of 2024 – and Mediobanca securities, resulting in the parties collectively surpassing the 25% ownership threshold in Piazzetta Cuccia.

This would have triggered the obligation to launch a takeover bid (OPA) for the institution, then headed by Alberto Nagel, an obligation which was reportedly not fulfilled.

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

UniCredit: Banco BPM's Market Value and Strong Shareholder Make New Bid Unfeasible, Says Orcel
data.description:
The market value of Banco BPM and the presence of Crédit Agricole as the bank's main shareholder represent a barrier to any new takeover attempt by UniCredit.

This was stated by Andrea Orcel, CEO of UniCredit.

Speaking before a parliamentary committee in Rome, Orcel said that, although in life nothing can ever be completely ruled out, the "BPM chapter is closed" for UniCredit.

Orcel reiterated that the legal battle his bank is pursuing over the terms set by the government for a deal on BPM--which UniCredit blames for the failed bid--aims to provide clarity and refute the notion that UniCredit could pose a threat to national security.

(Translated by Agnese Napoletti, editing by Stefano Bernabei)

data.title: Rheinmetall Not Currently Negotiating Conversion of Volkswagen Plant
data.description:
The defense group Rheinmetall is not currently in talks with Volkswagen regarding a conversion of the automaker's Osnabrück facility from civilian to military production.

At present, Rheinmetall has sufficient in-house capacity to fulfill existing orders, CEO Armin Papperger said Thursday at the Association of Foreign Press in Germany. However, he noted that this situation could change should the company receive additional large-scale contracts. During a visit to Volkswagen's Osnabrück plant, Papperger said he had already determined that certain components there would be suitable for military goods production.

Rheinmetall is gradually shifting parts of its civilian automotive production towards the rapidly expanding military sector. The group could also take over facilities from automakers such as Volkswagen if the conditions were right, Papperger has previously stated.

The defense industry has been awarded multi-billion euro contracts following Russia's invasion of Ukraine. As a result, Rheinmetall is experiencing rapid growth and is investing in new plants and acquisitions. The DAX-listed company is currently expanding its portfolio with the military division of the Bremen-based Lürssen shipyard and is establishing its own naval division. However, Papperger added that further acquisitions to strengthen the business are not currently planned.

(Reporting by Miranda Murray; editing by Matthias Inverardi; edited by Ralf Banser. For inquiries, please contact our newsroom at berlin.newsroom@thomsonreuters.com (for politics and economy) or frankfurt.newsroom@thomsonreuters.com (for companies and markets).

data.title: Fritz Kola Exits Family Business Association Over Openness to AfD
data.description:
HAMBURG (dpa-AFX) - Following in the footsteps of Rossmann and Vorwerk, Hamburg-based beverage producer Fritz Kola is also leaving the Association of Family Entrepreneurs. The company told the German Press Agency that the reason for its departure is the association's willingness to engage in dialogue with the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party. "An open, democratic society is the foundation for our economic and social actions. Against this backdrop, we have ended our membership in the association," Fritz Kola stated.

Fritz Kola will seek new ways to remain in dialogue with other entrepreneurs and maintain its presence as an economic player. Previously, both the drugstore chain Rossmann and home appliance manufacturer Vorwerk had already terminated their memberships.

dm Rejects Blanket Condemnation of AfD

In October, the association hosted a parliamentary evening at a Deutsche Bank branch in Berlin, inviting AfD representatives for the first time. Association president Marie-Christine Ostermann told "Handelsblatt" that the "ban on contact" with AfD Bundestag members had been lifted.

The drugstore chain dm also announced it is no longer a member. "We declared our exit many months ago and are therefore no longer part of the internal opinion-forming process," dm CEO Christoph Werner told the "Süddeutsche Zeitung." Whether the exit was related to the association's political stance remains unclear. Werner, however, opposes a blanket condemnation of the AfD, which the Federal Office for the Protection of the Constitution classifies as confirmed right-wing extremist.

He advocated for "factual and in-depth engagement with the issues." "dm rejects a polarizing firewall debate just as firmly as it rejects positions of the AfD party that call the free democratic basic order into question," the dm chief emphasized. According to the "Süddeutsche Zeitung," Werner explicitly wants to distinguish between the party itself and its anti-democratic positions.

Verdi: Stand Up to the Beginnings!

In contrast, Verdi chairman Frank Werneke called on employer and business associations to take a clear stance. The association under president Marie-Christine Ostermann risks "drifting definitively to the right," Werneke told the Funke Media Group newspapers. "History teaches us how important it is for the business community to draw a clear line against right-wing extremists." He pointed to historical parallels with industrialists' support for the Nazi party in 1933. "So: Stand up to the beginnings!" he said.

Berlin entrepreneur Harald Christ has also left the association. "I left the association because I take a clear stand for a free, open, and democratic economic policy," he told dpa. Recent developments and certain positions within the association no longer, in his view, maintained the necessary distance from political forces that are incompatible with these core values. The portal "The Pioneer" reported on Christ's exit several weeks ago.

DGB Calls for Support of the Firewall

Anja Piel, a member of the executive board of the German Trade Union Confederation (DGB), called on the association's member companies to stand clearly behind the firewall against the AfD. She told the Funke newspapers that tearing down this barrier "to the right" endangers democracy, international reputation, and Germany as a business location. According to her, the AfD's isolationist policies significantly hinder efforts to secure skilled labor and threaten the operational capability of hospitals and care facilities, for example.

She also noted that family businesses have a special historical responsibility, referencing the entanglement of German companies during the Nazi era.

Association Opposed to AfD in Government

The association defended its course. Discussing with those who think differently does not mean accepting their positions, Ostermann said. At the same time, the association does not want a government involving the AfD, as its worldview does not align with the association's commitment to freedom and a market economy.

This week, the association also released a statement. The hope that a quarter of German voters could be swayed by moral ostracism has not materialized, said president Ostermann. "Now, only engagement with the AfD's content, beyond simple categorizations into good and evil, can help."/cr/DP/he

data.title: Caltagirone, Milleri, and Lovaglio Under Investigation Over MPS Bid for Mediobanca - Corriere della Sera
data.description:
Francesco Gaetano Caltagirone, Delfin chairman Francesco Milleri, and Banca Monte dei Paschi CEO Luigi Lovaglio are under investigation for market manipulation and obstructing regulatory authorities, according to a report today by Corriere della Sera.

The investigation, led by the Milan Prosecutor's Office, concerns Monte dei Paschi's attempted takeover of Mediobanca, the newspaper writes.

There have been no comments so far from the individuals involved, and Reuters has not yet been able to verify the report published by Corriere.

(Reporting by Antonella Cinelli, editing by Stefano Bernabei)

data.title: Dutch prosecutor fines Morgan Stanley 101 million euros for tax evasion
data.description:
AMSTERDAM (Reuters) -The Dutch Public Prosecutor (OM) has imposed a total fine of 101 million euros ($117.10 million) on two Morgan Stanley entities in London and Amsterdam for dividend tax evasion, it said on Thursday.

The penalty is separate from the tax liability Morgan Stanley settled with the Dutch Tax Administration at the end of 2024, including accrued interest, the OM added.

Under Dutch law, domestic shareholders can reclaim or offset dividend taxes, but foreign recipients usually cannot. According to OM, Morgan Stanley used a special structure that allowed parties who were not eligible to improperly claim these tax rebates.

Earlier this year, the OM said it would summon Morgan Stanley but just before the start of criminal proceedings, the financial services company agreed to accept the fine.

Morgan Stanley said it was "pleased to have resolved this historical matter", which concerned corporate tax returns filed in the Netherlands more than 12 years ago.

($1 = 0.8625 euros)

(Reporting by Toby Sterling, Writing by Charlotte Van Campenhout; Editing by Kirsten Donovan)
